steps.title: Set up the weight inequality
steps.content:
Let $n$ = number of crates. The combined weight cannot exceed 5,180 pounds:
$$600 + 130n \leq 5,180$$

steps.title: Solve for the maximum number of crates
steps.content:
Subtract 600 from both sides:
$$130n \leq 4,580$$
Divide by 130:
$$n \leq \frac{4,580}{130} \approx 35.23$$
Since $n$ must be a whole number, the maximum is 35 crates. With 35 crates: $600 + 130(35) = 5,150$ pounds, within the limit. With 36 crates: $600 + 4,680 = 5,280$ pounds, exceeds the limit. That matches choice A.
